spec_match.rationale: Pinpoints the exact path the v137 rename touches (cross-origin sandboxed iframe + no activation) and renders the matrix the spec PR itself describes.

interactive.rationale: Two real lanes — same-origin and sandboxed iframe — each runs the WebAuthn call and classifies the caught error via postMessage.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb promises a side-by-side same-origin vs sandboxed probe; the page is exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the headline path but does not exercise the third dimension — a cross-origin frame *with* activation — even though it appears in the matrix table; running it would close the loop.

references.rationale: Links the correct chromestatus feature id 5160752715137024 and the spec PR #281.

design_system.rationale: All colours from variables, sensible accent-emerald/rose for spec-aligned vs legacy outcomes, no raw hex.

openQuestions.title: Add a third lane: cross-origin iframe WITH activation
openQuestions.detail: The matrix table lists three rows but only the first two have runnable buttons. Wire a third lane that requires the user to click a button inside the sandboxed iframe, then call create() with that fresh activation — this is the path that resolves successfully and would let the user confirm the rename only fires on the missing-activation branch.

openQuestions.title: Surface the failure mode if the sandboxed iframe cannot reach SPC
openQuestions.detail: If the browser blocks the postMessage roundtrip or the inline script in srcdoc is CSP-rejected, the lane silently stays on `loading sandboxed iframe...`. Add a timeout that flips to a clear `iframe never reported back` state with a hint about CSP.

openQuestions.title: Distinguish missing-activation from declined-prompt
openQuestions.detail: Both produce errors but only the missing-activation path is the one v137 renamed. Show in the verdict which physical condition the browser hit (no activation, user dismissed, etc.) so authors don't conflate the two.

summary: This is the strongest concept in the feature — it actually exercises the exact code path the rename targets via a sandboxed iframe. Only gaps are the missing `with activation` lane and a richer error classification.
